title: Seeded Asteroid Generation, Polygons, Sizes, and Difficulty Inputs · Behavior Case: Size Tier
summary: Random points self-intersect, seeds are global and hidden, small rocks award less but move impossibly, spawns overlap the ship, and difficulty grows without bound. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the size tier increment in seeded readable asteroid factory. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Define large, medium, and small radius, speed, score, fragment, rendering, and collision contracts.
Generate ordered angular vertices with bounded radius variation and validate minimum size, winding, and intersections.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-generation-polygons-difficulty-5
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-generation-polygons-difficulty-5.
# Case 9036: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def worked_asteroids_generation_polygons_difficulty_5(vertices: int = 11, min_radius: float = 18.0, max_radius: float = 31.0, seeded_equal: bool = True, self_intersections: int = 0, spawn_budget: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "playerworked5",
        "seed9036",
        "input5dekrow",
        "frameworked59036",
        "state9036worked5",
        "playtest5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if vertices < 5 or min_radius <= 0.0 or max_radius < min_radius:
        return False, vertices, "polygon-domain"
    if not seeded_equal:
        return False, vertices, "rng-drift"
    if self_intersections > 0:
        return False, self_intersections, "invalid-polygon"
    if spawn_budget < 1:
        return False, spawn_budget, "spawn-budget"
    assert max_radius >= min_radius
    return True, spawn_budget, "seeded-asteroids"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Asteroids decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected frame, input, seam, collision, cue, save, package, or release case, then state which native pygame-ce, SDL, display, controller, audio, filesystem, GPU, accessibility playtest, installer, performance, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

# Evidence: asteroids-size-tier-1huhr1

        "frame1huhr14832",

    if vertices < 5 or min_radius <= 0.0 or max_radius < min_radius:

    if not seeded_equal:

    if self_intersections > 0:

    if spawn_budget < 1:
        return False, spawn_budget, "spawn-budget"
    assert max_radius >= min_radius
    return True, spawn_budget, "seeded-asteroids"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Size Tier model · Theory Size Tier
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Size Tier: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 2239 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to generate readable seeded asteroid polygons under geometry, size, spawn, and difficulty budgets; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Define large, medium, and small radius, speed, score, fragment, rendering, and collision contracts.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Size Tier model · Theory Size Tier.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Size Tier model · Theory Size Tier, keep Size Tier connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define large, medium, and small radius, speed, score, fragment, rendering, and collision contracts.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Size Tier model · Theory Size Tier, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Size Tier precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Size Tier model · Theory Size Tier, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 2239 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to generate readable seeded asteroid polygons under geometry, size, spawn, and difficulty budgets; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Define large, medium, and small radius, speed, score, fragment, rendering, and collision contracts.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Size Tier model · Theory Size Tier, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Scaling one sprite changes every physical and gameplay property correctly.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: asteroids-size-tier-1huhr1.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: asteroids-size-tier-1huhr1(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_asteroids_size_tier_1huhr1\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?vertices < 5)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not seeded_equal)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?self_intersections > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?spawn_budget < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
